chapter_title.en: Chapter 12 – The pregnant woman sees the blood

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/238)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/238)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ الْحُبْلَى قَدِ اسْتَبَانَ حَبَلُهَا تَرَى مَا تَرَى الْحَائِضُ مِنَ الدَّمِ قَالَ تِلْكَ الْهِرَاقَةُ مِنَ الدَّمِ إِنْ كَانَ دَماً كَثِيراً أَحْمَرَ فَلَا تُصَلِّ وَ إِنْ كَانَ قَلِيلًا أَصْفَرَ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا إِلَّا الْوُضُوءُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from one of his men, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5th or 6th Imamasws), said, ‘I asked himasws about the pregnant woman whose pregnancy is detected, sees what the menstruating woman sees, from the blood. Heasws said: ‘That is the spilling from the blood. If it was a lot of blood, being red, so she should not pray Salaat; but if it was a little (blood), being yellow, so there is nothing upon her, except for the ablution’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from certain persons of his narrators from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once asked one of the Imam, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, about the case of the pregnant woman whose pregnancy has become apparent, but finds what a woman experiences because of Hayd (menses) of blood discharge. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is a spilling of blood; if the discharge is a great deal and of red color, she must not perform Salah (prayer). She then needs to take Wudu’ only if it is of a small amount and of yellow color.’”
